country: New South Wales, Queensland, South Australia, Victoria, Western Australia
mostTitles: Carlton (16 Premierships), Collingwood (16 Premierships), Essendon (16 Premierships)
description: The Australian Football League is the highest level of Australian rules football in the country and the premier professional competition in the sport. It features clubs from multiple Australian states and territories and represents the top tier of elite football, attracting the strongest players, largest crowds, and biggest national coverage. The league began in 1897 as the Victorian Football League before expanding nationally, and today its Grand Final is one of Australia’s biggest annual sporting events, played at Melbourne Cricket Ground in front of more than 100,000 spectators.

AFL Sydney is the premier Australian rules football competition in New South Wales and represents the highest regular senior level of the sport in the Sydney metropolitan region. The league features clubs from across Sydney and surrounding areas, combining long-established football traditions with continued growth in a state where rugby codes dominate much of the sporting landscape. It plays an important role in developing players, coaches, and clubs in New South Wales, while also providing a strong community football culture and local rivalries that are central to the sport’s expansion in the state.

country: New South Wales, Queensland, South Australia, Victoria, Western Australia
description: The AFL Women’s competition is the highest level of women’s Australian rules football in Australia. It brings together elite female players from across the country and continues to grow rapidly in standard, support, and national significance as the leading women’s competition in the sport. Since launching in 2017, the league has expanded quickly, introduced new clubs across multiple states, and become one of the fastest-growing women’s sporting competitions in Australia.

country: Australian Capital Territory, New South Wales, Northern Territory, Queensland, South Australia, Tasmania, Victoria, Western Australia
description: AFL National Championships is Australia’s premier representative junior football tournament, bringing together the best under-18 talent from each state and territory to compete at national level. The championships play a critical role in AFL talent identification, giving elite young players the opportunity to represent their region against the country’s strongest emerging footballers. Closely watched by AFL recruiters, the competition is a major pathway toward the national draft and future elite careers.

The Queensland Australian Football League is the leading state-level Australian rules football competition in Queensland. It serves as the highest regular senior competition in the state outside the national system and includes many of Queensland’s strongest clubs and emerging football talent. Because rugby codes dominate much of Queensland, the league has played a major role in expanding Australian rules football in the state and helping develop players for higher representative pathways.

mostTitles: George Town Saints and Old Scotch Collegians Tristles (8 Premierships)
description: Northern Tasmanian Football Association is a regional Australian rules football competition in northern Tasmania, operating as an important part of the state’s community football structure. While the original NTFA dates back to 1886, the current competition was re-established in 1996 after competition restructuring in Tasmania, following earlier league mergers and changes to statewide football. More recently, the league expanded in significance after the closure of the Tasmanian State League, with northern clubs returning to regional competition. The NTFA now combines strong local rivalries, player development pathways, and deep community connection across northern Tasmania.

Northern Territory Football League is the premier Australian rules football competition in the Northern Territory and one of the country’s most distinctive leagues, played during the tropical wet season from October to March. Based primarily in Darwin, the league features strong community clubs, a fast style of football, and significant Indigenous representation, with many players progressing to elite state and national levels. The NTFL plays a major role in Northern Territory football culture, combining high-level competition with deep local identity and strong regional support.

The South Australian National Football League is South Australia’s premier football competition and one of the most historic leagues in Australian football. It has a strong identity, deep club traditions, and has long been recognised as one of the strongest state competitions outside the AFL. Many South Australians still view SANFL club rivalries as central to local football culture, and the league’s Grand Final regularly draws large crowds to Adelaide Oval.

Southern Football League is a regional Australian rules football competition based in southern Tasmania, centered around Hobart and surrounding communities. The league plays an important role in the state’s football structure, providing strong local competition, traditional club rivalries, and a pathway for players progressing through Tasmanian football. Following major football restructuring in Tasmania, the SFL became increasingly significant as southern clubs returned to regional competition, reinforcing its place in the state’s grassroots and senior football landscape.

country: New South Wales, Northern Territory, Queensland, Tasmania, Victoria
mostTitles: Calder Cannons and Sandringham Dragons (6 Premierships)
description: Talent League is Australia’s leading under-18 Australian rules football development competition, featuring elite junior players from Victoria and selected regions across the country. It serves as a major pathway to the AFL and AFLW, bringing together representative teams, academy programs, and top emerging talent in a high-performance environment. Formerly known as the TAC Cup and later NAB League, the competition plays a central role in identifying and preparing the next generation of elite footballers.

United States Australian Football League is the national governing body for Australian rules football in the United States, overseeing club competition, regional tournaments, and national championships across the country. Founded in 1997, the league has driven the growth of Australian football in America by supporting men’s and women’s clubs, developing local talent, and expanding participation nationwide. The USAFL combines competitive football with strong community culture and serves as the highest level of Australian rules football outside Australia in the United States.

country: New South Wales, Queensland, Tasmania, Victoria
description: The Victorian Football League is Victoria’s top state-level competition and plays a major role in the football pathway beneath the AFL. It includes standalone clubs and AFL-affiliated teams, providing a high standard of competition and a direct development pathway for players. Before becoming a national competition, the VFL itself was Australia’s top league, so many clubs in today’s state competition carry more than a century of football history.

The Western Australian Football League is the premier state football competition in Western Australia and has a long history of producing elite talent. It remains one of the strongest state leagues in Australia, with proud clubs, strong local rivalries, and deep football tradition. WAFL clubs have supplied generations of AFL players, and the competition remains highly respected for its physical style and strong supporter loyalty across Perth and regional Western Australia.
